physiologicalParams.name: Resting heart rate
physiologicalParams.driver: Regular walking and aerobic sessions improve cardiac efficiency

physiologicalParams.name: Blood pressure
physiologicalParams.driver: Lower-sodium, potassium-rich meals plus near-daily movement

physiologicalParams.name: Fasting glucose
physiologicalParams.expectedChange: Steadier readings, 2–5 mg/dL lower over 12 weeks
physiologicalParams.driver: High-fiber carbohydrates and post-meal walks blunt glucose spikes

physiologicalParams.name: LDL / HDL cholesterol
physiologicalParams.driver: Olive oil, oily fish, and soluble fiber shift the lipid profile

physiologicalParams.name: VO2max
physiologicalParams.driver: Brisk walking, dance, and water aerobics raise aerobic capacity

physiologicalParams.name: Body-fat percentage
physiologicalParams.driver: A modest calorie balance with protein at every meal

physiologicalParams.name: Muscle mass
physiologicalParams.driver: Twice-weekly resistance work plus about 77 g protein per day

physiologicalParams.name: Sleep quality
physiologicalParams.typical: 6–8 h, lighter and more fragmented with age
physiologicalParams.driver: Daytime activity, morning light exposure, and lighter dinners

meals.items: Greek yogurt with rolled oats, blueberries, a drizzle of honey, and walnuts
Lentil-barley vegetable soup; whole-grain roll dipped in olive oil; an orange
Hummus with carrot, cucumber, and bell-pepper sticks; whole-grain crackers
Baked salmon with lemon and dill, herbed quinoa, roasted zucchini and cherry tomatoes

exercise.activity: Brisk walk followed by a light dumbbell strength circuit

week.highlight: Oily fish plus fiber-rich lentils front-load omega-3s and soluble fiber for heart health.

meals.items: Two-egg omelet with spinach and mushrooms; whole-grain toast with avocado; a kiwi
Miso-glazed tofu bowl with brown rice, edamame, steamed broccoli, and pickled carrot
A banana with a small handful of roasted almonds
Chicken-ginger stir-fry with snow peas and peppers over brown rice; a mandarin

exercise.activity: Tai chi session with standing balance drills

week.highlight: A plant-forward Japanese-style day pairs tofu and edamame with balance-focused tai chi.

meals.items: Overnight oats with chia seeds, diced mango, and a spoonful of peanut butter
Black bean and roasted sweet-potato tacos on corn tortillas with cabbage slaw and salsa
Plain yogurt with sliced strawberries and a sprinkle of granola
Grilled shrimp with cilantro-lime brown rice, black beans, and avocado-tomato salad

week.highlight: Beans two ways add fiber and potassium; water aerobics delivers cardio without joint stress.

meals.items: Small stack of whole-grain pancakes with warm blueberries, chopped walnuts, and a light drizzle of maple syrup
Olive-oil tuna salad on whole-grain bread with lettuce and tomato; carrot sticks; an apple
Air-popped popcorn with a sprinkle of parmesan; a few mixed nuts
Roast turkey breast with mashed sweet potatoes, steamed green beans, and cranberry relish

exercise.activity: Resistance-band strength session with sit-to-stand practice

week.highlight: Classic comfort foods, portioned for steady energy, on a strength-focused day.

meals.items: Vegetable poha (flattened rice) with green peas and peanuts; a small low-fat lassi
Chana masala (chickpea curry) with brown basmati rice and cucumber raita
Roasted chickpea and nut snack mix; a ripe pear
Tandoori-spiced baked cod with yellow dal, sauteed spinach, and a small whole-wheat roti

week.highlight: Indian spices and legumes make a high-fiber day feel indulgent rather than restrictive.

meals.items: Whole-grain toast with ricotta, sliced strawberries, and honey; a low-fat milk latte
Caprese-style farro salad with tomatoes, mozzarella, basil, and white beans
A peach and a small handful of pistachios
Whole-wheat spaghetti with lean beef and tomato ragu; spinach side salad with balsamic vinaigrette

week.highlight: An Italian-style day shows pasta fits fine when it is whole-grain and paired with lean protein.

meals.items: Shakshuka (two eggs poached in tomato-pepper sauce) with warm whole-grain pita
Mezze plate: hummus, tabbouleh, olives, whole-grain pita, and cucumber-tomato salad
Low-fat yogurt with honey, walnuts, and a sliced apricot
Za'atar roast chicken breast with pearl couscous, roasted carrots, and a lemony green salad

exercise.activity: Gentle yoga and stretching, plus an easy neighborhood stroll

week.highlight: A gentle recovery day: easy movement, relaxed Middle Eastern flavors, and an earlier, lighter dinner.

micronutrients.name: Calcium
micronutrients.keySources: Yogurt, milk, ricotta, tofu, leafy greens

micronutrients.name: Magnesium
micronutrients.keySources: Nuts, whole grains, beans, leafy greens

nutrition: About 77 g of protein spread across all four meals helps counter age-related muscle loss.
25+ g of daily fiber from beans, oats, and vegetables supports digestion, cholesterol, and steady blood sugar.
Calcium- and vitamin-D-rich foods such as yogurt, ricotta, and oily fish help maintain bone density after menopause.
Potassium-rich, lower-sodium meals support healthy blood pressure.
Unsaturated fats from olive oil, nuts, and fish support heart and brain health.
exercise: Twice-weekly strength work stimulates muscle and bone, lowering fracture risk.
Tai chi and balance drills sharpen stability and reduce fall risk.
Brisk walks, dance, and water aerobics raise aerobic capacity while sparing the joints.
Regular movement improves insulin sensitivity and helps keep blood pressure in range.
A light Sunday keeps joints mobile and lets muscles fully recover.

notes: Designed for a healthy woman around 68: extra protein, calcium, and balance training matter most at this stage. Portions and pace should always feel comfortable — this sample week is educational, not personal advice.
